21.correct: The ozone hole was first discovered over Antarctica, where seasonal depletion of stratospheric ozone is most severe.

wrong.A: The Arctic shows some ozone thinning but the major hole was found over Antarctica.
wrong.C: The Indian Ocean is not where the ozone hole was discovered.
wrong.D: The Pacific Ocean is not the site of the discovery.

21.fact: The Montreal Protocol (1987) was adopted to phase out ozone-depleting substances like CFCs.

22.correct: Giddha is a folk dance that originated in Punjab, performed by women with clapping and 'bolian'.

wrong.A: Rajasthan is known for Ghoomar and Kalbeliya.
wrong.D: Gujarat is known for Garba and Dandiya.

22.fact: Giddha is the lively female counterpart of the male Bhangra.

23.correct: Chhath Puja is mainly dedicated to Surya Dev (the Sun God) and his consort Usha/Chhathi Maiya, with worship at sunrise and sunset.

wrong.A: Goddess Durga is worshipped during Navratri/Durga Puja.
wrong.B: Lord Shiva is the deity of festivals like Mahashivratri.
wrong.D: Lord Vishnu is not the focus of Chhath.

23.fact: Chhath is a major festival of Bihar, Jharkhand and eastern Uttar Pradesh, offering thanks to the Sun for sustaining life.

24.correct: In the Yajurveda tradition, the 'Adhvaryu' priests performed the physical acts of the sacrifice (yajna) and recited the formulae in a low murmur.

wrong.A: They did not compose new ragas.
wrong.B: They did not perform dance rituals.
wrong.D: They did not write treatises on music theory.

24.fact: The four chief priests of a Vedic sacrifice were the Hotr, Adhvaryu, Udgatr and Brahman.

25.correct: Angular diameter (in radians) = actual diameter / distance = 3,474 / 384,000 = about 0.009 radian.

wrong.B: 0.008 radian is slightly too small for this calculation.
wrong.C: 0.010 radian is a little too large.

25.fact: This tiny angular size (about half a degree) is why the Moon and Sun appear almost the same size in our sky.

26.correct: If a satellite's altitude increases, its orbital radius grows, so its orbital period increases (by Kepler's third law, a larger orbit takes longer).

wrong.B: The period increases, not decreases, with greater altitude.
wrong.C: There is a definite change; it does not stay the same.
wrong.D: The change is systematic (an increase), not random.

26.fact: Higher orbits are slower: a satellite farther from Earth travels at a lower speed and takes longer per revolution.

27.correct: With 90% efficiency, the actual energy used is the ideal energy divided by 0.9, i.e. about 1.1 times the ideal energy (more input is needed to account for losses).

wrong.B: 1.2 times would correspond to about 83% efficiency.
wrong.D: 0.9 times would wrongly imply less energy than ideal is used.

27.fact: Efficiency = useful output / total input, so lower efficiency means more input energy is required.

28.correct: The first Indian Premier League final in 2008 was held at the DY Patil Stadium, Navi Mumbai, where Rajasthan Royals beat Chennai Super Kings.

wrong.A: The M. Chinnaswamy Stadium, Bengaluru did not host the 2008 final.
wrong.B: Eden Gardens, Kolkata was not the 2008 final venue.
wrong.C: Wankhede Stadium, Mumbai did not host the first IPL final.

28.fact: Rajasthan Royals, captained by Shane Warne, won the inaugural 2008 IPL title.

29.correct: GSAT-7 (Rukmini) is the ISRO satellite that supports the Indian Navy's communication needs, providing secure connectivity over the maritime region.

wrong.A: GSAT-12 is a general communication satellite, not the naval one.
wrong.C: GSAT-29 is a high-throughput communication satellite for remote areas.
wrong.D: Cartosat-2 is an Earth-imaging satellite, not a communication satellite.

29.fact: GSAT-7A, a related satellite, similarly supports the Indian Air Force.

30.correct: India is set to host the 2027 (43rd) BRICS Summit in New Delhi.

wrong.A: Beijing, China hosted earlier summits but not the 2027 edition.
wrong.C: Moscow, Russia is not the host for this summit.
wrong.D: Johannesburg, South Africa hosted the 2023 summit.

30.fact: BRICS expanded in 2024 to include new members beyond the original five countries.

31.correct: Vasudevan Bhaskaran captained the Indian hockey team that won the gold medal at the 1980 Moscow Olympics.

wrong.A: Ajit Pal Singh captained the 1975 World Cup-winning team, not the 1980 Olympic side.
wrong.B: Zafar Iqbal was a player but not the 1980 captain.
wrong.C: Mohammed Shahid was a star player of that era but not the 1980 captain.

31.fact: The 1980 Moscow gold remains India's most recent Olympic gold in field hockey.

32.correct: Nitrogen is largely inert in the atmosphere because its molecules (N2) have a very strong triple bond, so it does not react easily under normal conditions.

wrong.A: Nitrogen does not chiefly absorb ultraviolet rays; that is ozone.
wrong.C: Nitrogen is abundant (about 78% of air), not rare.

32.fact: The strong N=N triple bond must be broken (e.g. by lightning or special bacteria) for nitrogen to combine with other elements.

33.correct: In 2025 the Maharashtra State Road Development Corporation (MSRDC) was honoured for the Maharashtra Samruddhi Mahamarg (expressway) project.

wrong.A: The Adani Group was not the awardee for this project.
wrong.B: The Dalai Lama is unrelated to this infrastructure award.
wrong.D: Prasar Bharati is the public broadcaster, not the awardee.

33.fact: The Samruddhi Mahamarg is a long expressway connecting Nagpur and Mumbai.

34.correct: India's relief response to the 2025 Myanmar earthquake was named Operation Brahma, sending aid and rescue teams.

wrong.A: Operation Samudra Setu was a 2020 sea evacuation during COVID-19.
wrong.B: 'Operation Sahayata' is not the name used for this response.
wrong.D: Operation Maitri was India's 2015 relief mission for the Nepal earthquake.

34.fact: India frequently leads such 'first responder' humanitarian missions in its neighbourhood.

35.correct: An 'Eco-Sensitive Zone' (ESZ) is a buffer zone around protected areas (national parks and wildlife sanctuaries) where harmful activities are regulated.

wrong.A: It is not simply a pollution-affected area.
wrong.B: It is not a mining zone; mining is restricted there.
wrong.C: Wildlife corridors are related but the ESZ specifically refers to buffer zones around protected areas.

35.fact: ESZs act as 'shock absorbers' for protected areas, with graded restrictions on industry and construction.

36.correct: The primary goal of the Atmanirbhar Bharat Abhiyan is to promote self-reliance across all sectors of the economy, boosting domestic capacity and manufacturing.

wrong.A: It seeks to reduce dependence on imports, not encourage them.
wrong.C: Subsidising oil prices is not its aim.
wrong.D: Reducing food waste is not its primary objective.

36.fact: Atmanirbhar Bharat ('Self-Reliant India') was announced in 2020, with a large economic-stimulus package.

37.correct: The Dhuandhar Falls are formed by the Narmada river at Bhedaghat (near Jabalpur, Madhya Pradesh), where it plunges through the Marble Rocks.

wrong.A: The Tapti does not form the Dhuandhar Falls.
wrong.B: The Kaveri forms other falls like Shivanasamudra.
wrong.D: The Godavari does not form Dhuandhar.

37.fact: 'Dhuandhar' means 'smoke cascade', from the mist thrown up by the powerful falls.

38.correct: In 2025 the Maritime Development Fund was allocated about Rs 25,000 crore to support the shipping and maritime sector.

wrong.B: Rs 10,000 crore is lower than the figure announced.

38.fact: The fund aims to boost India's shipbuilding, ports and overall maritime ecosystem.

39.correct: The Mughal emperor Akbar strengthened the empire and is famous for his administrative reforms and policy of religious tolerance (sulh-i-kul).

wrong.B: Jahangir is known more for art and justice than for major administrative reforms.
wrong.C: Aurangzeb is associated with expansion but also religious orthodoxy, not tolerance.
wrong.D: Humayun lost and regained the throne but did not consolidate it like Akbar.

39.fact: Akbar introduced the Mansabdari system and the Din-i-Ilahi, and abolished the jizya tax.

40.correct: A freely suspended current-carrying rectangular loop in a magnetic field rotates until its plane lies along (parallel to) the field lines - i.e. its magnetic moment aligns with the field - which is its stable equilibrium.

wrong.A: With its plane perpendicular to the field lines the loop experiences maximum torque, so it does not stay there.
wrong.B: It settles into a definite orientation, not a random one.
wrong.D: There is a definite stable position, contrary to this option.

40.fact: This aligning torque on a current loop is the basic principle behind moving-coil galvanometers and electric motors.
